text.tspan: 01 · ROTATE THE TREE, NOT THE PHONE
The same directed tree runs top-to-bottom. Journey depth becomes natural vertical scrolling instead of horizontal drift.
Depth grows downward—the direction phones already scroll. Breadth is compressed deliberately rather than pushed off-screen.
Root stays above descendants at every size.
Fit width is the stable default viewport.
Edge badges show where more tree exists.
VERTICAL DEPTH IS THE MOBILE DEFAULT
The hierarchy is unchanged; only its orientation responds to the screen.
02 · FOLD BREADTH WITHOUT FLATTENING THE TREE
Distant subtrees remain attached to their real parents as branch piles with researched/open counts.
Every hidden subtree has a visible anchor, name, and count. Expanding a pile replaces only that local region.
The path being followed never collapses.
They remain graph nodes—not a detached list.
Other depth bands keep their positions.
A pile always sits on the same edge as the subtree it represents.
A branch never jumps into place unexpectedly. Focus is an explicit action with breadcrumbs and a clear return path.
The user chooses Focus branch. Only then does the selected subtree become the centered root of a bounded mobile view.
The original location never becomes mysterious.
The branch still feels connected to the full tree.
Full tree is always one tap away.
Changing spatial context is safe when the user initiates it and sees how to return.
The same top-down tree has three visual densities. Labels disappear before nodes become impossible to tap.
Hierarchy, direction, active route, selection, and touch area remain stable across zoom levels.
Markers and branch piles provide shape and scale.
Topic names and short questions appear.
Full cards expose answer and branch actions.
At overview scale, compact truthful marks are better than microscopic cards.
Selecting and branching preserve the vertical hierarchy. Details rise from the bottom only after the tree establishes context.
One live research turn will create the ghost node above.
The new turn is previewed as a child before confirmation. The sheet explains consequences without covering the parent and siblings.
Parent and two directions stay visible.
The result's future location is concrete.
Action details do not replace the graph.
BRANCHING SHOULD FEEL LIKE GROWING THE TREE
The interface previews the structural change before it spends or commits anything.
